description: Redesign arxiv recommender into a local-first research triage desk: 5-page IA (Inbox/Queue/Library/Monitor/Settings), unified design system, modal-based interactions, SQLite-first state layer

userStories.title: Add 5-page top navigation to base_research.html
userStories.description: As a user, I need a clear top navigation so I can access Inbox, Queue, Library, Monitor, and Settings directly.
acceptanceCriteria: base_research.html renders a top nav bar with links: Inbox, Queue, Library, Monitor, Settings
Current active page link is visually highlighted
Nav is present on all pages that extend base_research.html
Search link is NOT in the top nav
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Completed. NAV_ITEM_CONFIG updated to 5 pages. CSS tone classes added. Old pages (Track, Explore, Insights) still have routes but are not in nav.

userStories.title: Remove Search from top nav
userStories.description: As a user, I expect Search to not be a top-level nav item per PRD.
acceptanceCriteria: Search does not appear as a nav link in base_research.html
Search is accessible as a button/drawer within Inbox (e.g. '继续探索' button)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Already satisfied by US-001 changes. Search removed from NAV_ITEM_CONFIG. Inbox has '围绕问题继续探索' button linking to /search.

userStories.title: Create Queue page template with status tabs
userStories.description: As a user, I need a dedicated Queue page so I can manage reading workflow separately from Inbox triage.
acceptanceCriteria: New file templates/queue_research.html extends base_research.html
Top nav shows Queue as active
Page header: 'Queue / 阅读流程'
Status tabs render: Inbox | Skim Later | Deep Read | Saved | Archived
Two-column layout: list panel left, detail panel right
Page loads without errors at /queue
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created queue_research.html with status tabs, two-column layout, and detail panel. Added /queue route.

userStories.title: Create Library page template with Collections/SavedPapers/History tabs
userStories.description: As a user, I need a Library page to organize my research assets.
acceptanceCriteria: New file templates/library_research.html extends base_research.html
Top nav shows Library as active
Page header: 'Library / 研究资产库'
Three tabs render: Collections | Saved Papers | History
Two-column layout: tabs/list left, detail right
Page loads without errors at /library
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Rewrote library_research.html with 3 tabs. Updated route handler.

userStories.title: Create Monitor page template with Authors/Venues/Queries/RecentHits tabs
userStories.description: As a user, I need a Monitor page to track authors, venues, and research queries in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: New file templates/monitor_research.html extends base_research.html
Top nav shows Monitor as active
Page header: 'Monitor / 长期关注'
Four tabs render: Authors | Venues | Queries | Recent Hits
Two-column layout: subscription list left, recent hits right
Page loads without errors at /monitor
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created monitor_research.html with 4 tabs and added /monitor route.

userStories.title: Create Settings page template with Profile/Sources/Ranking/System tabs
userStories.description: As a user, I need a Settings page to manage research profile and preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: New file templates/settings_research.html extends base_research.html
Top nav shows Settings as active
Page header: 'Settings / 偏好与配置'
Four tabs render: Profile | Sources | Ranking | System
Each tab shows relevant form sections
Page loads without errors at /settings

userStories.notes: Rewrote settings_research.html with 4 tabs. Updated route to pass tab parameter.

userStories.title: Streamline Inbox left rail - remove excess modules
userStories.description: As a user, I want Inbox to focus only on triage. Left rail must not show collection count, saved search count, or complex asset summaries.
acceptanceCriteria: Inbox left rail shows ONLY: date switcher, filters (All/Untriaged/Relevant/Ignored/Queued), daily themes, job status
Left rail does NOT contain: collection count, saved search count, complex asset summaries, or monitor links
command-link-row section removed from inbox hero
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add More actions dropdown to Inbox detail panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want secondary actions (Add to Collection, PDF, BibTeX) hidden behind a More actions menu so the UI stays clean.
acceptanceCriteria: Inbox detail panel has a 'More actions' button
Clicking reveals: Add to Collection, Download PDF, Export BibTeX, View full explanation
These 4 actions are NOT visible as primary buttons
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add /queue and /library routes to web_server.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Flask routes to serve the new page templates.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /queue renders queue_research.html
GET /library renders library_research.html
Both routes pass necessary context (queue counts, collections, etc.)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add /monitor and /settings routes to web_server.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Flask routes to serve the Monitor and Settings pages.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /monitor renders monitor_research.html
GET /settings renders settings_research.html
Both routes pass necessary context (subscriptions, profile, etc.)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Collection modal/drawer component to base template
userStories.description: As a user, I want a proper modal to create a Collection instead of a browser prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: base_research.html includes a modal/drawer template for creating collections
Modal has fields: name (required), description (optional), seed query (optional)
Modal shows existing collections as selectable options
'Create new' and 'select existing' are two distinct flows in the modal
No window.prompt() is used for collection creation anywhere
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Queue page reads papers from SQLite state_store
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Queue page to display papers with their reading status from the state store.
acceptanceCriteria: Queue page reads from SQLite via state_store.py (not JSON/Markdown files)
Each status tab shows papers with matching queue_status
Clicking a paper shows detail in right panel
Status tabs show paper count badge
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Queue page supports moving paper between status
userStories.description: As a user, I want to move a paper from one reading status to another in Queue.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail panel has buttons for: Inbox / Skim Later / Deep Read / Saved / Archived
Clicking a status button updates paper's queue_status in SQLite via API
Paper moves to correct tab list immediately after save
Toast confirms the action
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Queue page supports adding and editing notes on papers
userStories.description: As a user, I want to add a personal note to a queued paper.
acceptanceCriteria: Detail panel has a note textarea field for selected paper
Note saves to SQLite on blur or explicit save button
Note persists when paper is re-selected
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Library Collections tab shows collection list and detail
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and manage my research collections.
acceptanceCriteria: Collections tab lists all collections: name, paper count, updated_at

'New collection' button opens the modal (not prompt)

userStories.title: Library Saved Papers tab shows papers with Saved status
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all papers saved for later in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: Saved Papers tab shows papers where queue_status = 'Saved'
Each paper shows: title, authors, date, citation count
Papers can be moved to a Collection from this view

acceptanceCriteria: Tab label reads 'Queries' not 'Saved Searches'

userStories.title: Extract service layer from web_server.py into services/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want recommendation, queue, library, and monitor logic extracted from web_server.py into separate modules.
acceptanceCriteria: services/recommendation_service.py handles recommendation logic
services/queue_service.py handles queue operations
services/library_service.py handles collections and saved papers
services/monitor_service.py handles subscriptions
web_server.py imports from services/ and does not contain direct business logic
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create viewmodels/ layer for page presentation models
userStories.description: As a developer, I want viewmodels to separate page data construction from Flask routes.
acceptanceCriteria: viewmodels/inbox_viewmodel.py builds Inbox page context
viewmodels/queue_viewmodel.py builds Queue page context
viewmodels/library_viewmodel.py builds Library page context
viewmodels/monitor_viewmodel.py builds Monitor page context
viewmodels/settings_viewmodel.py builds Settings page context
web_server.py routes call viewmodels instead of building context directly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add CSS spacing scale and remove ad-hoc alignment hacks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want consistent spacing so pages don't need min-height/calc patches.
acceptanceCriteria: CSS defines spacing scale: --space-8: 8px, --space-12: 12px, --space-16: 16px, --space-20: 20px, --space-24: 24px, --space-32: 32px
All margin/padding in pages use these variables
No new ad-hoc calc() or min-height added for alignment
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Unify status chip naming and styling across all pages
userStories.description: As a user, I want consistent status labels everywhere: Relevant/Ignored/Skim Later/Deep Read/Saved/Archived.
acceptanceCriteria: All pages use same 7 status labels (no 'liked', 'favorite', 'saved' as separate labels)
StatusChip component used consistently across Inbox, Queue, Library
Filter chips (All/Untriaged/Relevant/Ignored/Queued) look consistent everywhere
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Inbox scrolls paper list independently without page scroll
userStories.description: As a user, I want to scroll the paper list in Inbox without moving the whole page.
acceptanceCriteria: Inbox paper list is inside a scrollable container with max-height
Left rail and right detail panel stay fixed while center list scrolls
Three panels remain visually aligned at top as user scrolls
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Unify Modal, Drawer, EmptyState, and Toast components
userStories.description: As a developer, I want reusable modal/drawer/empty-state/toast components used consistently across all pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Modal component used for: create collection, create query subscription, confirm dialogs
Drawer component used for: add to collection flow, more actions
EmptyState component used when: no papers in queue, no collections, no subscriptions
Toast used for all action confirmations
No page implements its own inline modal/prompt/toast
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
